title: Domain-Driven Design published
description: Eric Evans published Domain-Driven Design: Tackling Complexity in the Heart of Software — the foundational text that gave the industry a shared vocabulary for modeling complex domains. It formalized bounded contexts, aggregates, entities, value objects, and ubiquitous language as first-class concepts. Two decades later, every serious domain modeling effort still starts from Evans's building blocks.
sddConnection: SDD inherits the entire building block vocabulary. Gap categories Structural Gap (SG) and Language Gap (LG) derive directly from Evans's concepts — SG catches missing or malformed aggregates and entities, LG catches ubiquitous language violations between specification and implementation.

title: CQRS + Event Sourcing patterns
description: Greg Young formalized Command Query Responsibility Segregation and Event Sourcing as complementary patterns for building event-driven systems. CQRS separates read and write models; Event Sourcing persists state as an append-only sequence of domain events. Together they gave architects a way to build systems where every state transition is explicit, traceable, and replayable.
sddConnection: SDD's specification model is event-sourced by design — each pass produces an immutable gap report, and the resolution log records every state transition. Commands, events, and projections appear as first-class building blocks in the architecture palette, directly inheriting Young's vocabulary.

title: Implementing DDD published
description: Vaughn Vernon published Implementing Domain-Driven Design — the tactical companion to Evans's strategic text. Vernon introduced the small aggregate heuristic, which argues that aggregates should be as small as possible while still protecting invariants. The book bridged the gap between Evans's conceptual framework and the practical realities of building DDD systems in production.
sddConnection: Vernon's small aggregate heuristic directly informs SDD's Heuristic Gap (HG) threshold defaults. When a gap report flags an aggregate that protects too many invariants or spans too wide a consistency boundary, it's applying Vernon's principle as a measurable diagnostic signal.

title: EventStorming formalized
description: Alberto Brandolini introduced EventStorming — a collaborative workshop format for domain discovery using sticky notes on a wall. The technique democratized domain modeling by making it visual, physical, and accessible to non-technical stakeholders. EventStorming became the de facto discovery tool for DDD practitioners, replacing whiteboard sessions with a structured, repeatable process.
sddConnection: SDD's architecture palette inherits the sticky note vocabulary — events, commands, aggregates, policies, and read models map directly to Brandolini's color-coded categories. Where EventStorming is a discovery tool for teams, SDD's gap report is a verification tool for solo practitioners. Discovery finds the domain; verification proves it's complete.

title: Bounded Context Canvas
description: Nick Tune created the Bounded Context Canvas — a structured template for documenting context boundaries, inbound and outbound communication, and the ubiquitous language within each context. The canvas gave teams a lightweight, visual tool for making strategic design decisions explicit and reviewable, filling a gap between EventStorming's discovery output and implementation.
sddConnection: The Bounded Context Canvas established the precedent for structured DDD tooling — a template that makes implicit design decisions visible. SDD follows the same philosophy: the canvas is a design tool that documents what you decided; the gap report is a diagnostic tool that tells you what you missed.

title: "DDD isn't done" keynote
description: At Explore DDD 2018, Eric Evans delivered a keynote arguing that Domain-Driven Design is not a finished body of knowledge — it's an evolving discipline with open problems. He called on the community to push DDD forward, identifying gaps in how practitioners handle complexity at scale, integrate with modern architectures, and verify that their models actually work.
sddConnection: SDD is a direct answer to Evans's call. The open problem Evans identified — how do you know your domain model is complete? — is exactly what gap reports solve. SDD provides a definition of done for the domain model itself: zero unresolved gaps across three passes.

title: Collaborative Software Design
description: Kenny Baas-Schwegler advanced collaborative software design practices by integrating cognitive bias awareness into domain modeling workshops. His work highlighted how confirmation bias, anchoring, and groupthink silently corrupt domain models — problems that no amount of technical skill can fix if the modeling process itself is flawed.
sddConnection: Gap reports surface the biases that informal review misses. A solo practitioner can't rely on team dynamics to catch blind spots, so SDD replaces social correction with structural diagnostics — the gap report is an automated second opinion that flags what confirmation bias would otherwise hide.

title: Learning DDD published
description: Vlad Khononov published Learning Domain-Driven Design — modern DDD pedagogy that made tactical and strategic patterns accessible to a broader audience. The book distilled two decades of DDD evolution into a clear, progressive curriculum, lowering the barrier to entry without sacrificing rigor. It became the recommended starting point for developers new to DDD.
sddConnection: Khononov's clarity of explanation influenced how SDD presents its building blocks and gap categories. The principle that complex ideas deserve simple explanations runs through both works — SDD's gap report format aims for the same accessibility that Khononov brought to DDD itself.

title: Narrative-Driven Development
description: Xolvio introduced Narrative-Driven Development and the temporal vocabulary — the moment primitive and temporal scope for domain events. NDD argued that domain events aren't just things that happened; they have temporal structure, lifecycle phases, and narrative arcs that traditional modeling ignores.
sddConnection: SDD inherits the moment primitive and temporal scope from NDD's vocabulary. Domain events in an SDD specification carry temporal metadata — when they can occur, how long they're valid, and what lifecycle phase they belong to. NDD's insight that events have narrative structure directly shaped how SDD models event lifecycles.

title: LLMs + DDD keynote
description: At Explore DDD 2024, Eric Evans explored the intersection of large language models and domain-driven design. He argued that LLMs can participate in domain modeling — not as replacements for human judgment, but as tools that can surface patterns, inconsistencies, and gaps that humans miss. The keynote signaled a major shift in how the DDD community thinks about AI.
sddConnection: Evans reached the same conclusion SDD had been building toward independently: AI-assisted domain modeling is viable, but only with structured guardrails. The convergence validates SDD's approach — using structured templates and gap reports as the interface between human domain expertise and AI analytical capability.

title: Balancing Coupling published
description: Vlad Khononov published Balancing Coupling in Software Design — a book that reframes coupling as a measurable, manageable property rather than something to blindly minimize. Khononov introduced coupling metrics and thresholds, arguing that the goal isn't zero coupling but the right coupling for your context.
sddConnection: SDD's gap thresholds follow the same philosophy — coupling and cohesion aren't binary pass/fail; they're spectrums with context-dependent thresholds. The Heuristic Gap (HG) category directly applies Khononov's principle: flag when a metric crosses a threshold, not when coupling merely exists.

title: AI Components for Deterministic Systems
description: Eric Evans presented on using Anti-Corruption Layers between deterministic domain systems and probabilistic AI components. The core insight: AI outputs are inherently probabilistic, but domain logic demands determinism — so you need a translation boundary that validates, constrains, and structures AI output before it touches the domain model.
sddConnection: SDD independently built the same ACL pattern. Structured specification templates serve as the anti-corruption layer between AI-generated analysis and domain truth. The gap report validates AI output against deterministic rules before any recommendation enters the resolution log.

title: Context Mapping with AI
description: Eric Evans presented 'Context Mapping with an AI-based Component' — applying strategic DDD patterns to the problem of integrating AI into existing domain architectures. The talk formalized how context maps should treat AI services: as separate bounded contexts with explicit translation layers, not as omniscient oracles embedded in the domain.
sddConnection: The same ACL pattern SDD built independently, now formalized by Evans himself. The convergence between Evans's strategic approach and SDD's template-based approach validates that structured boundaries between AI and domain logic are the correct architectural pattern.

title: LLM + Strategic Design workshops
description: DDD Europe 2026 featured dedicated workshops on integrating LLMs with strategic design — a sign that the community has moved from skepticism to serious engagement with AI-assisted modeling. Multiple workshop tracks explored how LLMs can participate in EventStorming, context mapping, and specification validation.
sddConnection: The community is mainstreaming the same ideas SDD has been building on since its inception. What started as a solo-practitioner experiment is now a recognized direction for the entire DDD community — AI as a modeling partner, not a replacement.

title: Signal-Driven Development published
description: Signal-Driven Development published — a solo-practitioner methodology that introduces three-pass convergence, gap reports as diagnostic signals, and zero unresolved gaps as the definition of done for domain specifications. SDD synthesizes two decades of DDD evolution into a rigorous, repeatable process that a single architect can execute with AI assistance.
sddConnection: This is the methodology you're reading about right now. Every node on this timeline contributed something SDD inherited, extended, or answered. The lineage isn't decoration — it's the proof that SDD stands on solid ground.
